name: DialogRoot
description: Root controller for a modal dialog. Uses native <dialog> element for top-layer rendering, focus trapping, and backdrop.
whenToUse: Modal interactions requiring user attention (confirmation, forms, detail views).
avoidWhen: Non-blocking information - use Popover. Destructive confirmations - use AlertDialog.
accessibilityRequirements: DialogContent requires aria-label or aria-labelledby. Focus is automatically trapped by native <dialog>.

parts.name: DialogTrigger
parts.description: Button that opens the parent Dialog. Renders a semantic <button> with aria-haspopup='dialog'.
parts.whenToUse: Always inside a DialogRoot to provide the open trigger.

parts.name: DialogContent
parts.description: Content panel of a Dialog. Uses native <dialog> with showModal(). Requires aria-label or aria-labelledby for accessibility.
parts.whenToUse: Always inside a DialogRoot to provide the dialog content.
parts.accessibilityRequirements: Must have aria-label or aria-labelledby (enforced by ModalLabelProps type).

parts.name: DialogClose
parts.description: Button that closes the Dialog when clicked.
parts.whenToUse: Inside DialogContent, typically in DialogFooter or as a close button in the header.

parts.name: DialogHeader
parts.description: Header section of a Dialog containing title and description.
parts.whenToUse: At the top of DialogContent to provide consistent header layout.

parts.name: DialogTitle
parts.description: Title heading of a Dialog. Announced by screen readers when the dialog opens.
parts.whenToUse: Inside DialogHeader. Always required for accessible dialogs.
parts.accessibilityRequirements: Required for accessible dialogs. Use aria-labelledby on DialogContent pointing to this title.

parts.name: DialogDescription
parts.description: Description text for a Dialog providing additional context.

parts.name: DialogFooter
parts.description: Footer section of a Dialog containing action buttons.
parts.whenToUse: At the bottom of DialogContent, typically containing confirmation and cancel buttons.

examples.title: Usage
examples.code:
{ const el = e.currentTarget as any; dialogOpen = (el.open ?? dialogOpen); }} onchange={(e) => { const el = e.currentTarget as any; dialogOpen = (el.open ?? dialogOpen); }} onclick={(e) => { const el = e.currentTarget as any; dialogOpen = (el.open ?? dialogOpen); }} ontoggle={(e) => { const el = e.currentTarget as any; dialogOpen = (el.open ?? dialogOpen); }}> Edit Profile Make changes to your profile here. Click save when you're done.

Dialog body content goes here.

examples.title: Feature Flag
examples.code: Dialog machine can be toggled with featureFlags.dialogMachine.
